issues.title: Epic: UI baseline standardization with Pico CSS for SSR pages
issues.description:
Goal:
- Introduce one shared UI baseline for server-rendered pages without SPA rewrite and without business-logic changes.

Scope:
- Pico as baseline + project tokens + bridge primitives.
- Shared document shell and static assets layer.
- Progressive migration of forms, dashboard, detail pages, and dispatch board shell.

Out of scope:
- React/Vue adoption.
- Business workflow redesign.
- Full dispatch board rewrite.

Success criteria:
- [ ] No new inline style islands in SSR page renderers.
- [ ] Shared shell is used by dashboard/forms/detail/dispatch pages.
- [ ] Dispatch board DnD and Event Calendar behavior remain intact.
- [ ] Local and Render verification/evidence updated and passing.

issues.title: PICO-1 ADR: SSR UI baseline strategy (Pico + bridge layer, no classless global switch)
issues.description:
Deliverables:
- One ADR/tech note describing exact rollout model.

Decisions to lock:
- Pico is baseline CSS only (no blind classless override across all pages).
- SSR HTML-string architecture remains unchanged.
- Dispatch board remains a special-case page with isolated overrides.

Acceptance:
- [ ] ADR exists and is linked from runbook/docs.
- [ ] Explicitly lists what is intentionally out of scope.

issues.title: PICO-2 Platform: static assets layer for CSS/JS/fonts under /assets
issues.description:
Implement:
- Add Express static middleware for `public/assets/*`.
- Create deterministic asset folders (`vendor`, `css`, optional `fonts`).
- Replace page-level CSS embedding dependencies with linked asset files where applicable.

Acceptance:
- [ ] `GET /assets/...` works in local and Render environments.
- [ ] Pico baseline is loaded from local assets, not inline or CDN.

issues.title: PICO-3 UI infra: shared SSR document shell helper for all page renderers
issues.description:
Implement:
- Add shared renderer helper (for doctype/html/head/body/meta/title/common links).
- Migrate `dashboardPage`, `pageFormShared`, `detailPage`, `dispatchBoardPageTemplate`, and `workOrderLifecyclePage` to use this shell.

Acceptance:
- [ ] One source of truth for document structure and global stylesheet/script links.
- [ ] Page renderers no longer duplicate full HTML shell boilerplate.

issues.title: PICO-4 Design system layer: tokens.css + app.css bridge primitives
issues.description:
Implement:
- Add `tokens.css` for shared semantic variables (surface, text, accent, warning, danger, spacing, radius, shadow).
- Add `app.css` primitives (`page-shell`, `page-header`, `panel`, `callout`, `action-bar`, `status-badge`, `table-wrap`, `empty-state`, etc.).
- Map existing ad-hoc variables (`--bg`, `--surface`, `--accent`, `--font`) into one canonical set.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Shared token set is referenced by all migrated pages.
- [ ] At least 8 reusable primitives replace duplicated page-specific definitions.

issues.title: PICO-5 Forms foundation: migrate pageFormShared.js to Pico-first markup and external CSS
issues.description:
Implement:
- Remove the large inline `<style>` from `pageFormShared.js`.
- Keep existing business helpers and validation UX while switching to shared shell + linked CSS + Pico-friendly semantics.
- Preserve lookup, entity selection, summary cards, field errors, and submit-busy behavior.

Acceptance:
- [ ] No inline page-wide style block remains in `pageFormShared.js`.
- [ ] Existing appointment/work-order form flows remain functionally equivalent.

issues.title: PICO-6 Forms rollout: align appointment and work-order lifecycle pages to shared primitives
issues.description:
Implement:
- Migrate `appointmentBookingPage.js` and `workOrderLifecyclePage.js` to shared primitives and shell contracts.
- Remove per-page visual divergence and local style islands.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Both pages render through shared shell/styles only.
- [ ] Error callouts, tables, action buttons, and section layouts are standardized.

issues.title: PICO-7 Dashboard migration: replace dashboard inline style block with shared UI baseline
issues.description:
Implement:
- Remove dashboard-specific large inline `<style>` block.
- Rebuild header/KPI/actions/tables using shared shell/tokens/primitives.
- Keep dashboard content and behavior unchanged.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Dashboard visual patterns match form pages.
- [ ] Existing dashboard API/UI assertions still pass after adaptation.

issues.title: PICO-8 Detail pages: migrate detailPage.js and residual standalone page styles
issues.description:
Implement:
- Remove remaining isolated style islands from detail-like pages (`detailPage.js` and any fallback branches in lifecycle pages).
- Reuse shared shell and bridge primitives for consistency and maintainability.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Detail/not-found style definitions no longer duplicate bespoke CSS blocks.
- [ ] Navigation/actions styling matches baseline conventions.

issues.title: PICO-9 Dispatch board integration: shared shell/topbar alignment with protected Event Calendar behavior
issues.description:
Implement:
- Keep dispatch board as special-case screen.
- Move only safe shared concerns (shell/topbar/base buttons/typography) to common layer.
- Keep board-specific layout and interaction styling isolated where required.
- Add explicit overrides for Pico baseline conflicts.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Drag/drop scheduling and reverse unassign continue to work.
- [ ] Calendar event readability/status highlighting remains intact.
- [ ] Board looks visually aligned with the rest of the app.

issues.title: PICO-10 Dependency hardening: vendorize Event Calendar CSS/JS and remove CDN runtime dependency
issues.description:
Implement:
- Replace Event Calendar CDN links in dispatch template with local pinned assets under `/assets/vendor`.
- Document upgrade process for vendor asset bump.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Dispatch page does not depend on public CDN availability at runtime.
- [ ] Version pin remains explicit and reproducible in repo.

issues.title: PICO-11 UI dedup: normalize button/panel/callout/table patterns + lightweight cookbook
issues.description:
Implement:
- Eliminate duplicated `.btn/.panel/.callout` definitions where shared primitives suffice.
- Create a compact cookbook (markdown or internal page) with canonical usage patterns.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Each common UI pattern has one recommended markup/style path.
- [ ] New pages can be implemented without copying CSS from existing renderers.

issues.title: PICO-12 Verification and evidence: update tests/scenarios/render checks for new UI baseline
issues.description:
Implement:
- Update tests that assert legacy inline CSS fragments.
- Run full relevant gate (`lint`, `test`, `verify`, `scenario:dispatch-board`, `verify:render` as applicable).
- Capture updated evidence artifacts/screenshots for dashboard, booking, work-order, dispatch board.
- Update `STATUS.md`, `PLANS.md`, and affected docs (`11`, `23`) to reflect the new UI baseline path.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Verification suite passes with updated assertions.
- [ ] Evidence artifacts are attached and reviewable.
- [ ] State documents reflect accepted rollout status and caveats.
